name: Surescripts E-Prescribing & Medication Intelligence API
what_it_does: Surescripts REST API for national e-prescribing and medication intelligence network. Enables AI agents to route electronic prescriptions to pharmacies, access patient medication history across the national network, retrieve real-time drug benefit and formulary information, handle prior authorization electronic workflows (ePA), access specialty medication data and hub services, integrate electronic prior auth with payers, retrieve prescription routing and delivery confirmation, and connect EHR and pharmacy systems via the national prescription network.
use_cases: Routing electronic prescriptions from EHR to pharmacies from ePrescribing automation agents
Accessing patient medication history from medication reconciliation agents
Retrieving formulary and drug benefit data from clinical decision support agents
Managing electronic prior authorization for medications from payer integration agents
not_for: Consumer applications without clinical workflow integration
Non-HIPAA covered entities without clinical data sharing agreements
best_when: An EHR vendor, pharmacy system, or health IT platform wants to integrate national e-prescribing routing, medication history, and electronic prior authorization via the Surescripts network.
avoid_when: HIPAA+DEA: Prescription data is PHI and controlled substance prescriptions require DEA EPCS compliance. Medication history includes sensitive health information across the patient's pharmacy history. EPCS (Electronic Prescribing for Controlled Substances) has strict DEA certification requirements.

auth.notes: Surescripts uses OAuth 2.0 and API key for API access. Organization-level credentials with network participant agreements required. Certification process required for network participation. Scopes control prescribing, medication history, and formulary access. EPCS requires additional DEA certification.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — national network transaction-based pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-transaction pricing for prescription routing, medication history, and formulary queries
pricing.notes: Arlington, Virginia. Founded 2001 as joint venture of NCPDP, NACDS, and APA. National e-prescribing network owned by pharmacy and healthcare industry stakeholders. Processes 20B+ prescription transactions annually. Regulatory scrutiny from FTC. Mandatory network for Medicare Part D e-prescribing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per network participant agreement; high-volume capable

known_agent_gotchas: HIPAA+DEA: Prescription data PHI; EPCS requires DEA certification for controlled substances
No public MCP server — network participant agreement required before API access
National network participant certification required — not open to general developers
EPCS (Electronic Prescribing for Controlled Substances) has strict DEA audit trail requirements
Medication history covers entire national pharmacy network — highest-sensitivity patient data
Medicare Part D mandates e-prescribing via Surescripts — critical infrastructure for Part D plans
agent_readiness.security_notes: National prescription network PHI. HIPAA, DEA EPCS, SOC2, HITRUST. OAuth2. US data residency. Network participant agreement. Highest security tier in e-prescribing.

security_score_components.security_notes: National prescription PHI. HIPAA, DEA EPCS, SOC2, HITRUST. OAuth2. US data residency. Critical healthcare infrastructure.
